scope.summary: F-LOW DC6 verified already satisfied: the driver's integer parse rejects every exponent form before prefix scanning, so Oracle's max-negative NUMBER sentinel -1e126 fails closed instead of collapsing to -1. This close records verification, not new work.
out_of_scope: Any code change, in either repo. The strtoll-style collapse the finding describes is already gone.
Float64 coercion, which deliberately retains -1e126 and is asserted separately in the same test.
The documented decimal-fraction truncation for ordinary numerics, which the bead requires to stay unchanged and which is untouched.

entry_points.name: parse_number_i64 / parse_number_u64 (rust-oracledb crates/oracledb/src/arrow/builders.rs)
entry_points.trace: Both reject b'e'/b'E' before the prefix digit scan (builders.rs:107-109, 131-133). The guard sits in the shared parse function that all 5 call sites use, so the bead's 'both row and columnar Int64 paths' are covered by construction rather than by two separate patches. arrow::tests::number_sentinel_preserves_float_and_rejects_integer_collapse passes under --features arrow: it decodes the real single-byte [0x00] sentinel (a decoder-produced fixture, not hand-assembled), asserts Float64 keeps -1.0e126, asserts the Int64 target fails closed, and asserts -1 and 12 still map while 1e3 is refused. Bound to driver commit 5ed87b09ef39 by file digest in the in-scope handoff artifact.

limitations: Verification of pre-existing state: I read the cited code and ran the test; I did not re-derive which commit replaced the strtoll-style scan.

Not mutation-checked. The fix is not mine and the existing test was written against it; I confirmed the guard is on the shared parse path rather than injecting a fault into another repo's committed code.
The 'row path' in the bead's wording is covered by the shared parse function rather than by a separate row-oriented assertion.
Scoped verification: cargo test -p oracledb --lib (347 passed) and --features arrow for the sentinel test.
